# UC-19.1.14 · UCS Manager Backup Validation

## Description

Confirms scheduled all/configuration backups completed and file size is within expected bounds.

## Value

Confirms scheduled all/configuration backups completed and file size is within expected bounds.

## Implementation

Ingest backup job results from syslog or automation. Alert on failed job or zero-size artifact.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`, backup scheduler logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:ucs:backup`, syslog backup events.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest backup job results from syslog or automation. Alert on failed job or zero-size artifact.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:backup" earliest=-7d
| where status!="success" OR backup_size_bytes < 1000000
| stats latest(status) as st, latest(backup_size_bytes) as sz by ucsm_host
| table ucsm_host, st, sz
```

Understanding this SPL

**UCS Manager Backup Validation** — Confirms scheduled all/configuration backups completed and file size is within expected bounds.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:ucs:backup`, syslog backup events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`, backup scheduler logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_ucs; **sourcetype**: cisco:ucs:backup. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_ucs, sourcetype="cisco:ucs:backup", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where status!="success" OR backup_size_bytes < 1000000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ucsm_host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Pipeline stage (see **UCS Manager Backup Validation**): table ucsm_host, st, sz


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco UCS Manager (faults, equipment, and service profile context) for the same DNs and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (backup status), Single value (failed jobs), Timeline.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:backup" earliest=-7d
| where status!="success" OR backup_size_bytes < 1000000
| stats latest(status) as st, latest(backup_size_bytes) as sz by ucsm_host
| table ucsm_host, st, sz
```

## Visualization

Table (backup status), Single value (failed jobs), Timeline.
